title: Conformance Defect-Code Vocabulary
description: Schema for conformance/errors.json. NON-NORMATIVE: this validates a testing artifact, not a CDX document. It lives outside schemas/ deliberately — everything in schemas/ describes normative CDX structures.

normative.description: Always false: this vocabulary is never part of the specification.

version.description: The vocabulary's own version; bumps on any addition or deprecation.

specVersion.description: The CDX specification version the clause citations resolve against.

summary.description: What the defect is. MUST describe every branch of the guard that emits it.

clause.description: The specification clause defining the requirement.

disposition.description: The load-time disposition the specification assigns (State Machine section 5.4). null means the specification assigns none — not that a consumer may ignore it.

dispositionClause.description: The section 5.4 row relied on, so a reviewer can verify the disposition.

outcome.description: Lineage only (09 section 3.3). A REJECTED outcome is NOT the REJECT disposition.

deprecated.description: Retired codes keep their entry forever; the vocabulary is append-only.

then.description: A non-null disposition MUST cite the section 5.4 row it was taken from, so a reviewer can verify it rather than trust it.
